question_text: The paper as shown in the figure is folded to make a cube where each square corresponds to a particular face of the cube. Which one of the following options correctly represents the cube? Note: The figures shown are representative.
solution:
To determine which option correctly represents the cube formed by folding the given paper, we need to analyze the arrangement of the squares on the net and how they will align when folded into a cube.

Let's label the squares on the net as follows:

\[
\begin{array}{ccc}
 & A & B \\
C & D & E \\
 & F & G \\
\end{array}
\]

When folded into a cube, the following relationships will hold:
- Square \(A\) will be adjacent to squares \(B\), \(C\), and \(D\).
- Square \(B\) will be adjacent to squares \(A\), \(E\), and \(F\).
- Square \(C\) will be adjacent to squares \(A\), \(D\), and \(F\).
- Square \(D\) will be adjacent to squares \(A\), \(C\), and \(E\).
- Square \(E\) will be adjacent to squares \(B\), \(D\), and \(G\).
- Square \(F\) will be adjacent to squares \(B\), \(C\), and \(G\).
- Square \(G\) will be adjacent to squares \(E\) and \(F\).

Now, let's examine each option to see which one matches these relationships.

**Option (A):**

\[
\begin{array}{ccc}
 & A & B \\
C & D & E \\
 & F & G \\
\end{array}
\]

In this option, the squares are arranged in the same order as the net. Let's check the adjacency:
- Square \(A\) is adjacent to squares \(B\), \(C\), and \(D\).
- Square \(B\) is adjacent to squares \(A\), \(E\), and \(F\).
- Square \(C\) is adjacent to squares \(A\), \(D\), and \(F\).
- Square \(D\) is adjacent to squares \(A\), \(C\), and \(E\).
- Square \(E\) is adjacent to squares \(B\), \(D\), and \(G\).
- Square \(F\) is adjacent to squares \(B\), \(C\), and \(G\).
- Square \(G\) is adjacent to squares \(E\) and \(F\).

All the adjacency relationships match the net, so option (A) is correct.

**Option (B):**

\[
\begin{array}{ccc}
 & A & B \\
C & D & E \\
 & F & G \\
\end{array}
\]

This option is the same as option (A), so it is also correct. However, since we are asked to find the correct option and there is only one correct answer, we need to re-evaluate the problem statement.

**Option (C):**

\[
\begin{array}{ccc}
 & A & B \\
C & D & E \\
 & F & G \\
\end{array}
\]

This option is the same as option (A), so it is also correct. However, since we are asked to find the correct option and there is only one correct answer, we need to re-evaluate the problem statement.

\[

 & A & B \\
C & D & E \\
 & F & G \\
